IOT_PROXY.TITLE: LAN HTTP proxies
IOT_PROXY.INTRO: Register devices on your home network behind this app. Reach them from the browser via PatTool — only private IPv4 LAN addresses are allowed upstream.
IOT_PROXY.PASS_LEAVE_BLANK: Leave blank to keep the saved password unchanged.
IOT_PROXY.SERVER_SETTINGS_UNAVAILABLE: Could not load server limits. Use Refresh and try again.
IOT_PROXY.SETTING_SECRET_CONFIGURED: Dedicated secret configured (32+ characters, recommended for production)
IOT_PROXY.SETTING_SECRET_DEFAULT: Auto-derived at startup (set env IOT_PROXY_HMAC_SECRET / app.iot-proxy.open-token-hmac-secret for a stable key)
IOT_PROXY.SETTING_MAX_REWRITE: Max body size for LAN path rewriting in HTML/JS

LOTTO.TITLE: French Loto (FDJ) draws
LOTTO.INTRO: Draws come from the server MongoDB: they load automatically when you open this page; you can also use “Reload table” anytime. Pick a start and end month (inclusive), then run import — by default the end is the current calendar month (capped at the archive’s last month when needed) and the start is the previous month. Archive coverage is October 2008 through May 2026. Administrators only.
LOTTO.INVALID_RANGE: Start month must be before or equal to end month.
LOTTO.FILTER_EMPTY: No draws match this date range.
LOTTO.EMPTY: No draws in the database yet; an admin can run the import.
LOTTO.LOAD_ERROR: Could not load draws from the server.
LOTTO.LOGIN_REQUIRED: Sign in to start the import (the server will crawl the archives for several minutes).
LOTTO.SYNC_ADMIN_ONLY: Archive import is restricted to accounts with the Administrator (Admin) role.
LOTTO.SYNC_ADMIN_TOOLTIP: Only users with the Admin role can run this import.
LOTTO.SYNC_DONE: Import finished: {{months}} months processed, {{draws}} draws saved (HTTP errors: {{errors}}).
LOTTO.DATE_SAVE_FORBIDDEN: Saving is restricted to administrators (or your session expired).
LOTTO.DATE_EDIT_TOOLTIP: Turn draw date editing on or off (admin). Dates are read-only until you start editing.
LOTTO.JSON_AI_TOOLTIP: Assistant: pre-aggregated `pat-loto-ai-v2` (counts + tail). Export modal keeps full recordCount/draws.
LOTTO.JSON_AI_MODAL_HINT: Readable export: root recordCount, draws[] (drawDate, numbers, chance). The assistant button sends precomputed histogram JSON (smaller prompt).
LOTTO.AI_FAB_TOOLTIP: Opens the assistant with the **new quantitative prompt** + **pre-aggregated** statistics JSON (`pat-loto-ai-v2`). You send the message when ready.
LOTTO.AI_PROMPT_FROM_SCRATCH: **French Lotto — human-readable answer.** Data = **`pat-loto-ai-v2`** below (UI pre-counted histograms): **`c`={{n}}**, `mf` for mains **1–49**, `cf` for Chance **1–10**, **`chi2*Naive`**, **`tail`**.** Mandatory order:** **(1) First lines must be grids** — **GRID_A** then **GRID_B**, each **one readable line**: **five ascending numbers + Chance**, e.g. `Numbers: 4, 11, 22, 35, 41 — Chance: 7`. Then **one sentence**: model fit to the JSON, not the official draw. **(2) Short method:** **maximum five** bullet lines, each ≤one short sentence (**no** sub‑paragraphs or nested headings). **(3) Fair play** — one sentence.** Presentation:** PatTool renders **Markdown** — use a `##` heading for the grids block; **bold** every number inside **GRID_A** / **GRID_B**; separate sections with `---`; use `-` bullet lines for the method; put the lottery disclaimer in a `>` blockquote (renders tinted). Optionally a **tiny** GFM table (header plus **only** the two slips); **never** a large statistical table.** Do not** paste raw `<html>` / `style=` (**stripped by the app**, so colours come from Markdown structure alone). **Still forbidden:** dumping every main number with stats; long maths **before** the grids; decorative RNG.
LOTTO.AI_STATS_PAYLOAD_INTRO: **Attached aggregate JSON (`pat-loto-ai-v2`)** — reason from histograms plus `tail`, not kilometric per-draw transcripts.
LOTTO.AI_JSON_BLOCK_INTRO: Compact JSON (fewer tokens): `c` = authoritative count = **`d.length`**. Each `d[i]` = `[ "YYYYMMDD", [5 numbers], chance ]` chronological.
LOTTO.AI_RECORD_COUNT_LINE: Authoritative count: **{{n}}** (= JSON `c`; must equal **`d.length`**). Do not infer from how many tuple rows you see — long JSON can be truncated. If `c`≠visible `d` length, say so and analyse only the visible slice.
LOTTO.AI_MESSAGE_1: Lotto draws in **compact** JSON below. Trust **`c`** and the bold line; use visible `d` tuples. If context is partial, say so. First apply the **most advanced statistics & probability** you reasonably can: marginals/joints for five numbers + Chance, entropy, spacing & rank summaries, cautious serial checks; **goodness-of-fit** vs fair-draw nulls (multinomial, etc.), **bootstrap / permutation**, **multiplicity control** when stacking tests; derive **combinatorial** win odds under official rules. **Show your work**: name each model/hypothesis and give **reproducible detail**—explicit formulas, aggregated counts from the visible history, test statistics and **p**-values / intervals (or bootstrap/permutation bands where used), hyperparameters (e.g. Dirichlet α), and optimisation objective + constraints for each constructed slip. **Then**, as a compulsory final section (e.g. “Model-derived play slips”), output **≥2 concrete candidate slips** (five numbers + Chance) that are **not** picked at random—each slip must cite how the numbers follow from the computations above (not a black box). Optionally a rough calibrated comparison to uniformity. Acknowledge briefly that in an ideal fair lottery no past-data estimator improves **intrinsic** win probability; slips are **explicit outputs** of your models, not claims of prophecy.
LOTTO.CHART_MODAL_HELP: For each calendar month: mean of the 5 numbers (after ascending sort: rank 1 = smallest drawn … rank 5 = largest), and mean of the Chance number — if several draws occur in the same month, averages pool all of them.
LOTTO.CHART_EMPTY: Not enough data to plot the chart (missing draws or numbers).
LOTTO.SOURCE_NOTE: Source: public data aggregated by LesBonsNumeros.com (not affiliated with FDJ). Verify on fdj.fr if needed.

EUROMILLIONS.TITLE: EuroMillions (FDJ) — draws
EUROMILLIONS.INTRO: Draws come from the server MongoDB. Admins use “Download FDJ ZIP + import”: the backend fetches the official February 2020+ archive from fdj.fr into euromillions.import.directory on the server, extracts the CSV files, and merges draws into MongoDB (FDJ draw code as key). Reload the table anytime. Ranking #1 payouts are summed from CSV columns where available.
EUROMILLIONS.FILTER_EMPTY: No draws match this date range.
EUROMILLIONS.EMPTY: No draws stored yet — an administrator must import the CSV bundle from the configured directory.
EUROMILLIONS.LOAD_ERROR: Could not load draws from the server.
EUROMILLIONS.SYNC_ADMIN_ONLY: CSV import is restricted to accounts with the Administrator (Admin) role.
EUROMILLIONS.SYNC_ADMIN_TOOLTIP: Only users with the Admin role can trigger the import.
EUROMILLIONS.FDJ_ARCHIVE_TOOLTIP: Admin: downloads the latest “from February 2020” ZIP from fdj.fr into euromillions.import.directory on the server, then imports CSV files into MongoDB.
EUROMILLIONS.FDJ_HISTORIQUE_SITE_TOOLTIP: Opens fdj.fr in a new tab: official history page containing the downloadable archive PatTool pulls from.
EUROMILLIONS.SYNC_DONE: Import finished: {{files}} CSV file(s) read, {{draws}} draws saved into MongoDB, {{skipped}} data row(s) skipped.
EUROMILLIONS.DATE_SAVE_FORBIDDEN: Saving is restricted to administrators (or your session expired).
EUROMILLIONS.DATE_EDIT_TOOLTIP: Turn draw date editing on or off (admin). Dates are read-only until you start editing.
EUROMILLIONS.JSON_AI_TOOLTIP: Assistant: `pat-eurom-ai-v2` (draws since {{since}}: aggregates + full chronological list in `tail`). Export modal: entire loaded history.
EUROMILLIONS.JSON_AI_MODAL_HINT: Readable export: recordCount, draws[] (full loaded history). The assistant sends every draw since **{{since}}** in `tail`, plus `periods` aggregates (server setting `euromillions.ai.min-draw-date`).
EUROMILLIONS.AI_FAB_TOOLTIP: **EuroMillions**: scratch prompt + **`pat-eurom-ai-v2`** (aggregates + **all** draws since {{since}} in `tail`). Send manually.
EUROMILLIONS.AI_PROMPT_FROM_SCRATCH: **EuroMillions — human-readable answer.** Data = **`pat-eurom-ai-v2`** below (PatTool pre-counts): scope **`sinceInclusive` = {{since}}** (server property `euromillions.ai.min-draw-date`); **`c`={{n}}** draws in that scope; **`periods[]`** per era (`mf` for mains **1–50** across **5×n** slots, `sf` for stars up to **starMax**, **`chi2*Naive`**), plus **`tail`** = **every** draw in that scope, chronological tuples `[YYYYMMDD,[5 mains],[★,★]]` (**`tail.length`** = **`c`**).** Mandatory order:** **(1) Lead with grids — zero preamble:** first printable lines must be **GRID_A:** one line, **five ascending mains + two ascending stars**, plain language e.g. `Main balls: 7, 12, 23, 34, 45 — Stars: 3, 9`. Then **GRID_B:** second deterministic line, same pattern. Then **one sentence**: these are **statistical model outputs from the JSON**, not the official next draw. **(2) Short method — max five bullet lines**, each one short sentence (**no** extra sections or long prose). **Forbidden:** dumping rows like `P… ball s=… obs=… smoothed_prob=` for dozens of indices (fills the reply and truncates before the user sees numbers). At most **one** χ² + **df** line if helpful. **(3) Fair play — one sentence:** draws are random; no prophecy.** Presentation:** PatTool renders **Markdown** (`##` / `###`, **bold**, bullets, horizontal rules, blockquotes for colour/tint in the UI). Structure the answer that way; **no** raw HTML/`style=`. A **small** GFM table (header + **only** **GRID_A**/**GRID_B** rows) is fine; **no** huge tables.** Always forbidden:** long numeric traces **before** GRID_A; decorative RNG.
EUROMILLIONS.AI_STATS_PAYLOAD_INTRO: **Attached JSON (`pat-eurom-ai-v2`)** — scope **`sinceInclusive`** (**{{since}}**): use `periods` + `tail` (**full** chronological list for that window; **`tail.length`** = **`c`**).
EUROMILLIONS.AI_JSON_BLOCK_INTRO: Compact JSON (fewer tokens): `c` = authoritative count = **`d.length`**. Each `d[i]` = `[ "YYYYMMDD", [5 main balls], [star1, star2] ]` chronological.
EUROMILLIONS.AI_RECORD_COUNT_LINE: Authoritative count: **{{n}}** (= JSON `c`; should equal **`tail.length`** in this schema). If the model context looks cut off, say so; otherwise **`c`** matches **`tail.length`**.
EUROMILLIONS.AI_MESSAGE_1: EuroMillions draws in **compact** JSON below. Trust **`c`** and the bold line; use visible `d` tuples. If context is partial, say so. First apply **state-of-the-art statistics & probability** on the visible history: empirical laws & dependence structure for mains + stars if testable; entropy/gaps/ranks; **GOF**, **bootstrap/permutation**, **multiplicity-aware** inference; regime changes if periods differ combinatorially. **Show your work**: for every major step give **reproducible calculation detail**—named hypotheses, explicit formulas, tabulated or summarised counts from the data you see, test statistics and **p**-values / CIs (or bootstrap/permutation bands), model hyperparameters, and the exact optimisation or selection rule + objective value that yields each slip. **Then**, compulsory final section (e.g. “Model-derived play slips”), output **≥2 full candidate combinations** (5 mains + 2 stars) constructed **deterministically** from that pipeline—each combination must trace back to those computations (**no** black-box picks). Briefly quantify under your model vs i.i.d. uniform if tractable; one sentence: fair-draw physics means claims of **edge** versus true randomness are usually unwarranted—deliver slips as **explicit outputs** of your estimators nevertheless.
EUROMILLIONS.CHART_MODAL_TITLE: Means by month — main balls & stars
EUROMILLIONS.CHART_MODAL_HELP: Per calendar month: mean of each sorted ball rank (balls 1–50 on the left axis) and separate means for Lucky Stars sorted as star #1/#2 (1–12, right axis). Multiple draws in the month are pooled into the averages.
EUROMILLIONS.CHART_EMPTY: Not enough tidy data for the chart.
EUROMILLIONS.MONTH_COUNT_MODAL_HELP: Each row is a calendar month (January→December, vertical axis). Each column is a year appearing from {{since}} onward (assistant lower bound, inclusive). A cell counts draws in that month and year. Scroll horizontally to browse all years.
EUROMILLIONS.MONTH_COUNT_SUMMARY: {{draws}} draw(s) placed in the grid from {{since}} (inclusive). {{skipped}} row(s) skipped (no yyyy-MM-dd day prefix). {{beforeBound}} draw(s) before {{since}} excluded from the grid. {{pairs}} month×year slot(s) with at least one draw; {{years}} year column(s).
EUROMILLIONS.MONTH_COUNT_EMPTY: Nothing to group by month among skipped rows — see counts (usually unreadable date prefix).
EUROMILLIONS.MONTH_COUNT_ALL_BEFORE_BOUND: Every loaded draw falls strictly before {{since}} (assistant lower bound). The grid is empty.
EUROMILLIONS.AI_MIN_DATE_SOURCE_PROPERTIES: Effective value: application.properties (no Mongo row yet).
EUROMILLIONS.SOURCE_NOTE: Source: CSV open-data bundle supplied by FDJ / government lottery statistics. Always confirm latest results on authorised FDJ outlets.

CALCULATOR.TITLE: Calculator
CALCULATOR.SUBTITLE: Standard arithmetic (+, −, ×, ÷). Enable “scientific calculator” for sin, cos, √, logs, π, and more.
CALCULATOR.KEYBOARD_HINT: Keyboard: digits; + − * /; Enter or = for equals; Esc clears all; Backspace deletes the last digit.
CALCULATOR.TAPE_MODE_LABEL: Detailed view (numbers and operators, one line each)
CALCULATOR.TAPE_EMPTY: Each number and operator will appear here.
CALCULATOR.SCIENTIFIC_ANGLES_HINT: Use the switch: trigonometry and inverse trig in degrees or radians.
CALCULATOR.SCI_TITLE_EXP: e raised to the power of x
CALCULATOR.SCI_TITLE_EXP10: 10 raised to the power of x
CALCULATOR.FORMULA_HINT: Use x as the variable. Examples: sin(x), x^2 + 3*x, sqrt(abs(x)), exp(-x^2), log(x, 10). Trigonometry here uses radians (multiply by pi/180 for degrees). Constants: pi, e. Definite integral ∫ₐᵇ f(x) dx uses composite Simpson’s rule (even number of subintervals). Presets labelled ∫… show antiderivatives to plot. Optional purple curve: cumulative ∫ from x min (trapezoids on samples; second Y axis).
CALCULATOR.FORMULA_INTEGRAL_A_ARIA: Lower limit a of definite integral
CALCULATOR.FORMULA_INTEGRAL_B_ARIA: Upper limit b of definite integral
CALCULATOR.FORMULA_INTEGRAL_PANELS_ARIA: Number of subintervals for Simpson rule (adjusted to even)
CALCULATOR.FORMULA_FS_MATH_BAR_ARIA: Buttons to insert math expressions into the formula
CALCULATOR.FORMULA_PLOT_INTEGRAL_HINT: Adds ∫ from x min to x using trapezoids on the same samples as the curve (second vertical axis). Restarts at 0 after gaps where f is undefined.
CALCULATOR.FORMULA_INTEGRAL_CURVE_CAPTION: Purple curve — cumulative ∫ from {{xmin}} to x of ( {{expr}} ), trapezoidal rule.
CALCULATOR.FORMULA_ERR_SYNTAX: Could not parse the expression. Check parentheses and function names.
CALCULATOR.FORMULA_ERR_NONREAL: Result is not a real number at this x (complex or undefined).
CALCULATOR.FORMULA_ERR_RANGE: Invalid interval: x max must be greater than x min.
CALCULATOR.FORMULA_ERR_NOPOINTS: No points could be plotted on this interval (domain errors or non-real values).
CALCULATOR.FORMULA_ERR_INTEGRAL_RANGE: Invalid limits: a and b must be finite with b greater than a.
CALCULATOR.FORMULA_ERR_INTEGRAL_FAIL: Numerical integration failed (non-real or undefined values on [a, b]).

ASSISTANT.TITLE: PatTool assistant
ASSISTANT.ROUTING_GROUP: AI provider and model for this assistant
ASSISTANT.BRAVE_SEARCH_HINT: Opens Brave Search (search.brave.com) in a new browser tab
ASSISTANT.FAB_REPLY_READY_TITLE: New reply ready — open the assistant
ASSISTANT.EMPTY: Ask a question. The conversation stays in this panel; messages are sent to the server only.
ASSISTANT.PLACEHOLDER: Message… (Enter to send, Shift+Enter for new line)
ASSISTANT.CLEAR_DRAFT_HINT: Clears the input field only (the visible conversation is kept).
ASSISTANT.PLACEHOLDER_NAMED: {{name}} — message… (Enter to send, Shift+Enter for new line)
ASSISTANT.FULLSCREEN_HINT: Expands the assistant panel to fill the screen.
ASSISTANT.EXIT_FULLSCREEN_HINT: Leaves fullscreen; the panel becomes a side drawer again.
ASSISTANT.NEW_CHAT_TITLE: Clear this tab’s chat history. The text in the input box is kept.
ASSISTANT.ASK_FROM_EVENT_TITLE: Open the assistant with this activity title as the message
ASSISTANT.ASK_FROM_NEWS_TITLE: Open the assistant with this news headline (web search enabled)
ASSISTANT.COPY_TO_INPUT_TITLE: Copy this question into the message field
ASSISTANT.COPY_QUESTION_HINT: Copy the text in the input field to the clipboard.
ASSISTANT.COPY_QUESTION_ERROR: Could not copy — check your browser permissions.
ASSISTANT.COPY_REPLY_TITLE: Copy this reply to the clipboard
ASSISTANT.CREDITS_TOGGLE: Open the OpenAI usage page on the platform in a new tab
ASSISTANT.CREDITS_TOGGLE_ANTHROPIC: Open Anthropic billing and usage in a new tab
ASSISTANT.CREDITS_TOGGLE_GEMINI: Open Google AI Studio usage and quotas in a new tab
ASSISTANT.CREDITS_BTN_HINT: Opens the OpenAI usage page in a new browser tab (URLs from server config).
ASSISTANT.CREDITS_BTN_HINT_ANTHROPIC: Opens Anthropic billing and usage in a new browser tab.
ASSISTANT.CREDITS_BTN_HINT_GEMINI: Opens Google AI Studio (usage and quotas) in a new browser tab.
ASSISTANT.CREDITS_ANTHROPIC_CONSOLE_LINE: Anthropic does not expose prepaid balance in PatTool. Manage billing and usage at {{url}} (sign-in required).
ASSISTANT.CREDITS_GEMINI_CONSOLE_LINE: Google does not show detailed usage inside PatTool. Usage and rate limits: {{rateLimitUrl}} · API keys: {{apiKeysUrl}} (Google sign-in required).
ASSISTANT.CREDITS_OPENAI_CONSOLE_LINE: Full billing detail is not shown entirely in PatTool. Billing: {{billingUrl}} · Usage: {{usageUrl}} (OpenAI sign-in required).
ASSISTANT.CREDITS_NETWORK_ERROR: Could not reach server for credits.
ASSISTANT.CREDITS_ERR_NO_RESPONSE: No response from the PatTool server. Check that the backend is running and that environment API_URL matches the Spring port (see README server.port — often http://localhost:8000/api/ in dev).
ASSISTANT.CREDITS_ERR_UNAUTHORIZED: Access denied (401/403). Sign in again or check your session.
ASSISTANT.CREDITS_ERR_NOT_FOUND: Credits endpoint not found (404). Check backend version and API_URL (expected …/api/assistant/openai/credits).
ASSISTANT.CREDITS_ERR_TOKEN: Could not obtain an auth token. Try again or sign in.
ASSISTANT.TOOLS_GROUP: OpenAI tool options for this request
ASSISTANT.TOOLS_OPTIONAL_HINT: Enables extra OpenAI capabilities beyond plain chat: web search, images, MCP server.
ASSISTANT.TOOL_WEB_HINT: The model can search the web for up-to-date information (slower, higher API usage).
ASSISTANT.TOOL_IMAGE_HINT: The model can generate images; may cost more and take longer.
ASSISTANT.TOOL_MCP_HINT: Connects an MCP server configured on the backend (openai.mcp.* settings).
ASSISTANT.TOOL_MCP_DISABLED_HINT: MCP unavailable: no MCP server is configured yet.
ASSISTANT.TOOLS_HELP_BTN_HINT: Rough guide to strong model choices for each kind of task
ASSISTANT.TOOLS_HELP_RANK_TABLE_CAPTION: Suggested model rankings by task type.
ASSISTANT.TOOLS_HELP_RANK_FOOTNOTE: Indicative guidance only — model names and availability change; your account and APIs matter.
ASSISTANT.NEW_THREAD_HINT: Clears the conversation in this tab. Does not erase the text you are typing.
ASSISTANT.HISTORY_HINT: Open conversations saved on the server for your account.
ASSISTANT.HISTORY_MODAL_INTRO: Saved after each assistant reply (debounced). Clear this tab’s chat with the eraser to start a new stored conversation.
ASSISTANT.HISTORY_COPY_QUESTION_TOOLTIP: Copy the first user question to the clipboard and into the message field.
ASSISTANT.HISTORY_COPY_QUESTION_OK: Question copied — clipboard and message field.
ASSISTANT.HISTORY_COPY_QUESTION_EMPTY: No user question text to copy for this conversation.
ASSISTANT.HISTORY_COPY_QUESTION_ERR: Could not load the conversation to copy the question.
ASSISTANT.HISTORY_DELETE_HINT: Remove this conversation from your history
ASSISTANT.HISTORY_DELETE_CONFIRM: Delete this conversation from the server? This cannot be undone.
ASSISTANT.HISTORY_SAVE_ERR: Could not save this conversation to the server (too large or network error).
ASSISTANT.SEND_HINT: Sends your typed message to the assistant; if an image is attached, the text must contain your question.
ASSISTANT.IMAGE_ATTACH_HINT: Attach an image (camera or gallery), then type your question above (JPEG, PNG, GIF, WebP, max 8 MB).
ASSISTANT.IMAGE_ATTACH_CAMERA_HINT: Take a new photo with the camera.
ASSISTANT.IMAGE_ATTACH_FILES_HINT: Choose an existing image from gallery, Google Photos / Files picker, etc. (JPEG, PNG, GIF, WebP, max 8 MB).
ASSISTANT.IMAGE_NEED_QUESTION: Type your question about the image in the message box before sending.
ASSISTANT.PLACEHOLDER_WITH_IMAGE: Ask your question about this image…
ASSISTANT.IMAGE_DEFAULT_PROMPT: Describe and analyze this image in detail.
ASSISTANT.IMAGE_TYPE_REJECTED: Use JPEG, PNG, GIF, or WebP.
ASSISTANT.IMAGE_READ_ERROR: Could not read this image file.
ASSISTANT.IMAGE_SENT_NOTE: Image sent (preview is not kept after reload).
ASSISTANT.TRANSCRIPT_PHOTO_LINE: This message includes a photo (see attachment).
ASSISTANT.SHARE_WHATSAPP_HINT: Export conversation: pick WhatsApp from the share sheet or open WhatsApp Web with the text.
ASSISTANT.EXPORT_PDF_HINT: Download this conversation as a PDF (provider and model shown for each assistant reply).
ASSISTANT.EXPORT_PDF_ERROR: Could not generate the PDF. Please try again.
ASSISTANT.WHATSAPP_NATIVE_SHARE_NOTE: On mobile (or desktops with native share), you can choose WhatsApp and send text plus images. Otherwise WhatsApp may open with the text only; very long transcripts are shortened.
ASSISTANT.SHARE_WHATSAPP_TEXTAREA_PLACEHOLDER: Add text only if you want…
ASSISTANT.SHARE_WHATSAPP_PREVIEW_ADDON_LABEL: Extra message (sent only when you write it)
ASSISTANT.SHARE_WHATSAPP_PREVIEW_HINT: The recap below is pasted into WhatsApp after the PatTool title and any optional extra you typed (no link is added).
ASSISTANT.SHARE_GENERATED_IMG_WHATSAPP_HINT: Shares this generated image on WhatsApp together with the text that was used to generate it.
ASSISTANT.SHARE_GENERATED_IMG_PROMPT_LABEL: Text used to generate the image
ASSISTANT.SHARE_GENERATED_IMG_WHATSAPP_CAPTION_HIN: The message text was copied to the clipboard: after sending the photo on WhatsApp, paste it as the caption (WhatsApp often ignores the text field when an image file is attached).
ASSISTANT.INSERT_IMG_IN_EVENT_HINT: Insert this generated image as an attachment of an existing event (stored in the database with the event's other files).
ASSISTANT.INSERT_IMG_IN_EVENT_DESC: Pick the event to add the generated image to. It will be stored alongside the event's other attachments.
ASSISTANT.INSERT_IMG_IN_EVENT_LOAD_ERROR: Could not load the events list.
ASSISTANT.INSERT_IMG_IN_EVENT_FILE_ERROR: Could not prepare the image file for upload.
ASSISTANT.INSERT_IMG_IN_EVENT_NO_USER: Not signed in; cannot insert the image.
ASSISTANT.INSERT_CMT_IN_EVENT: Add as comment to an event
ASSISTANT.INSERT_CMT_IN_EVENT_HINT: Insert this conversation (question + assistant reply) as a comment of an existing event. Also handy for image-recognition results.
ASSISTANT.INSERT_CMT_IN_EVENT_MODAL_TITLE: Add a comment to an event
ASSISTANT.INSERT_CMT_IN_EVENT_DESC: Review or edit the text below, then pick the event to add it to as a comment.
ASSISTANT.INSERT_CMT_IN_EVENT_TEXTAREA_PLACEHOLDER: Text of the comment to post on the event…

ABOUT.MONGODB_DESC: Cloud NoSQL document database with authentication
ABOUT.DISCUSSION_DESC: Real-time discussion via WebSocket (STOMP/SockJS) with backend persistence (Spring Boot).
ABOUT.EXCEPTION_REPORT_DESC: Send a report of all tracked user connections and exceptions with IP addresses from the last 7 days
ABOUT.REPORT_ACTIONS_SUBTITLE: Send by email, view or refresh the report in a single click.
ABOUT.STREAMING_ARCHITECTURE_DESC: Hybrid streaming system with client-side caching
ABOUT.STREAMING_OVERVIEW: The system uses a hybrid approach: backend streams ALL events once via Server-Sent Events (SSE), frontend caches ALL events in memory, and displays only 8 at a time with client-side pagination.
ABOUT.STREAMING_BACKEND_DESC: Backend streams all matching events from MongoDB 8.2 via SSE, one by one, in real-time.
ABOUT.STREAMING_FRONTEND_DESC: Frontend caches all events in memory. Scrolling uses cached data - NO backend calls.
ABOUT.STREAMING_BENEFITS_DESC: Fast scrolling with no network latency, instant display of next 8 events, offline-ready, instant search/filter on cached data.
ABOUT.STREAMING_APPROACH_DESC: Stream once, cache all, client-side pagination

SAVE_CACHE.TITLE: Data Maintenance
SAVE_CACHE.DESC: Executes maintenance tasks: saves compressed image cache and cleans up old Govee records (IoT thermometers). Govee records older than the configured retention period will be deleted.

STOCK.ERROR_UPSTREAM: Twelve Data service is temporarily unavailable.
